economic_notes: Splatsin has business enterprises including Yucwmenlucwu (Caretakers of the Land) 2007 LLP engaged in logging operations and environmental services (revenues $13.1M), Quilakwa Investments Ltd. operating a retail gas station (revenues $10.1M), Splatsin Development Corporation, Sexqeltkemc Enterprises Inc. and Sexqeltkemc Limited Partnership. Multi-Purpose Community Facility and gas bar/convenience store financed through FNFA for $18.26M. COVID-19 pandemic noted as subsequent event with uncertain impact.
notes: Fiscal year 2020. Auditor notes COVID-19 pandemic as uncertain future impact but no adjustments made. Splatsin uses fund accounting with four consolidated funds. Three business entities (Quilakwa Investments Ltd., Splatsin Development Corporation, Yucwmenlucwu 2007 LLP) accounted for by modified equity method. Sexqeltkemc Enterprises Inc. and Sexqeltkemc Limited Partnership accounted for by modified equity method as significant influence investments. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP held at cost. Secwepemc RFA accounted for by proportionate consolidation (expired). Accumulated remeasurement gains of $3,436,450 included in accumulated surplus. FNFA long-term debt of ~$15.1M for community facility and gas bar. Annual FNFA debt servicing costs $1,012,968. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided text. Segmented financial statement (Appendix 2) was cut off before completion.
